title: Exercises: Scaling Strategies for Quantum Applications

exercises.title: Implement Custom Load Balancer
exercises.description: Create a load balancing strategy that considers real-time calibration data.
tasks: Extend LoadBalancingStrategy to create a CalibrationAwareBalancer
Factor in time since last calibration (prefer recently calibrated backends)
Consider T1/T2 coherence times relative to circuit depth
Implement a scoring function that penalizes backends with stale calibration (>24h)
Test with a mock registry containing 5 backends with varying calibration times
exercises.expectedOutput: CalibrationAwareBalancer class with test demonstrating backend selection
hints: Recent calibration means more accurate error mitigation
Calibration drift is typically linear over short periods
Weight calibration freshness against queue time trade-offs

exercises.title: Build Shot Distribution Optimizer
exercises.description: Create an advanced shot distributor that minimizes total execution time.
tasks: Implement a ShotDistributionOptimizer that models backend throughput
Consider that different backends have different shot execution rates
Implement an optimization algorithm to minimize max(completion_time) across backends
Handle the constraint that some backends may have minimum shot requirements
Calculate and report the theoretical speedup achieved
exercises.expectedOutput: Optimized shot distribution with throughput modeling and speedup calculation
hints: This is a load balancing optimization problem
Consider using linear programming or greedy approximation
Account for both queue time and execution time

exercises.title: Circuit Partitioning Analysis
exercises.description: Analyze the trade-offs of circuit partitioning for different circuit types.
tasks: Create test circuits: linear chain (n qubits), star topology, fully connected
Partition each circuit type using the CircuitPartitioner
Calculate the overhead factor for each partitioning
Determine the maximum practical circuit size for each topology
Write a report comparing partitioning efficiency across topologies
exercises.expectedOutput: Analysis report with overhead calculations for each circuit topology
hints: Linear chains have minimal cut edges when partitioned
Fully connected circuits have many cut edges
The feasibility threshold is typically overhead_factor < 1000

exercises.title: Auto-Scaling Simulation
exercises.description: Simulate auto-scaling behavior under varying load conditions.
tasks: Create a workload simulator that generates time-varying metrics
Implement a simulation loop that evaluates scaling every minute
Generate a 1-hour simulation with peak load in the middle
Track scaling actions and resource utilization over time
Visualize the scaling behavior (text-based chart acceptable)
exercises.expectedOutput: Simulation output showing scaling actions over time with utilization metrics
hints: Use sine wave or step function to model load variations
Track both over-provisioning and under-provisioning events
